    <div class="ct-inner">		
						<div class="ct-main ct-main-post" id="ct-main" role="main">
					<div class="ct-post">
						<article id="post-75216" class="post-75216 post type-post status-publish format-standard has-post-thumbnail hentry category-tutorials tag-css-scroll tag-css-view tag-scroll">
							<header>
                <div class="ct-subline entry-meta">
								  <div class="ct-subline__author">
									    									        									            									                <span class="byline author vcard">
													    By <a href="https://tympanus.net/codrops/author/argyleink/">Adam Argyle</a>													</span>
									            									        									        in
									        <a href="https://tympanus.net/codrops/category/tutorials/">Tutorials</a> on 
									    									    <time class="ct-subline__pubtime" pubdate="pubdate">January 17, 2024</time>
									    									</div>
                </div>
								<h1>A Practical Introduction to Scroll-Driven Animations with CSS scroll() and view()</h1>
								<div class="ct-post-excerpt"><p>With just CSS you can add scroll animations that toggle shadows on navbars, reveal images, add scrollytelling, link up carousel elements and much more.</p>
</div>
                                <p class="ct-post-tags">
                  <a href="https://tympanus.net/codrops/tag/css-scroll/">css scroll</a> <a href="https://tympanus.net/codrops/tag/css-view/">css view</a> <a href="https://tympanus.net/codrops/tag/scroll/">scroll</a>                 </p>
                								
							</header>		
							<div class="ct-post-content">
								    <div class="ct-post-featured-img">
              <img width="800" height="600" src="https://codrops-1f606.kxcdn.com/codrops/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/scrolldrivencss-opt.gif?x72090" class="attachment-post-thumbnail size-post-thumbnail wp-post-image" alt="" decoding="async" fetchpriority="high">          </div>
  								<div id="tympa-2084672996" data-tympa-trackid="91142" data-tympa-trackbid="1" class="tympa-target tympa-target"><p class="ct-msponsor-article"><a style="display:flex" href="https://www.creativecodingclub.com/bundles/creative-coding-club?ref=0d0431" target="_blank" rel="nofollow sponsor noopener">Ready to become a GSAP expert? Access the world’s most comprehensive GSAP training with 300+ lessons. Enroll now →</a></p></div>								
<p>Let’s make a <a href="https://drafts.csswg.org/scroll-animations-1/">CSS scroll animation</a>! No frameworks, no JavaScript. Connect user interaction with real time scroll interaction feedback; helping transition color, position, visibility, and more. </p>



<p>At the time of writing this, it is Chromium only:</p>


<strong>Browser Support:</strong> <ul class="browser-support"><li class="bs-chrome bs-supported"><span class="bs-browser">Chrome</span><span class="bs-info">Supported</span></li><li class="bs-firefox"><span class="bs-browser">Firefox</span><span class="bs-info">Not supported</span></li><li class="bs-ie"><span class="bs-browser">Internet Explorer</span><span class="bs-info">Not supported</span></li><li class="bs-safari"><span class="bs-browser">Safari</span><span class="bs-info">Not supported</span></li><li class="bs-opera"><span class="bs-browser">Opera</span><span class="bs-info">Not supported</span></li></ul>



<p>I feel scroll animations are perfect for progressive enhancement, but you may consider using a <a href="https://github.com/flackr/scroll-timeline" data-type="link" data-id="https://github.com/flackr/scroll-timeline">polyfill</a> if it is crucial for them to function across all browsers today.</p>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">A humble spinning animation</h2>



<p>Let’s begin with something familiar—an infinitely spinning element. It has keyframes that rotate the star five times over five seconds, continuously:</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@keyframes</span> spin</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token selector">to</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">transform</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">rotateY</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>5turn<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span>

<span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@media</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token property">prefers-reduced-motion</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> no-preference<span class="token punctuation">)</span></span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token selector">div</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">animation</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> spin 5s ease infinite<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<figure class="wp-block-video"><video height="720" style="aspect-ratio: 1280 / 720;" width="1280" autoplay="" controls="" loop="" muted="" src="https://codrops-1f606.kxcdn.com/codrops/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/regular-spinning-star.mp4?x72090" playsinline=""></video></figure>



<p class="has-text-align-center"><a href="https://codepen.io/argyleink/pen/BaboeRb/13baeebe69e01667b8e5402cab808654">Try the demo</a></p>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">A scroll driven animation (SDA)</h2>



<p>Let’s <strong>convert</strong> that animation <strong>to a scroll-driven</strong> animation, if the browser supports it. </p>



<p>Add <strong>one line of CSS</strong> that instructs the animation to be triggered by scrolling, using the <a href="https://drafts.csswg.org/scroll-animations-1/#scroll-notation" data-type="link" data-id="https://drafts.csswg.org/scroll-animations-1/#scroll-notation" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener"><code class="" data-line="">scroll()</code></a> function:</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@media</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token property">prefers-reduced-motion</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> no-preference<span class="token punctuation">)</span></span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@supports</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">scroll</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span></span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token selector">div</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
      <span class="token property">animation</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> spin linear both<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
      <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">scroll</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
    <span class="token punctuation">}</span> 
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<figure class="wp-block-video"><video height="720" style="aspect-ratio: 1280 / 720;" width="1280" autoplay="" controls="" loop="" muted="" src="https://codrops-1f606.kxcdn.com/codrops/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/scroll-star.mp4?x72090" playsinline=""></video></figure>



<p class="has-text-align-center"><a href="https://codepen.io/argyleink/pen/abMvrdB/7425e042efa174aaf02c7f27501204a5">Try the demo</a></p>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">What just happened?!</h3>



<p>To the keyframes we defined, they still run from 0-100%. But now, <strong>0% is the scroll start</strong> position and <strong>100% is the scroll end</strong> position. You changed the <a rel="noreferrer noopener" href="https://drafts.csswg.org/css-animations-2/#propdef-animation-timeline" target="_blank">animation-timeline</a>.</p>



<p>Notice the addition of <code class="" data-line="">both</code>, an <a rel="noreferrer noopener" href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/animation-fill-mode" target="_blank">animation-fill-mode</a> value that allows the animation to play forwards and backwards.</p>



<p>I did add some supporting styles. I applied <code class="" data-line="">position: fixed</code> to the image, allowing us to observe its spin as it remains in the viewport. Additionally, I modified the animation easing to linear because, in my experience, scroll-linked animations tend to feel better when linearly connected to my scrolling gesture.</p>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">A scroll port intersection driven animation</h2>



<p>Next, change <a href="https://drafts.csswg.org/scroll-animations-1/#scroll-notation"><code class="" data-line="">scroll()</code></a> to <a href="https://drafts.csswg.org/scroll-animations-1/#view-notation"><code class="" data-line="">view()</code></a> and <strong>add one more line</strong> of CSS that specifies the <code class="" data-line="">animation-range</code>:</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@media</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token property">prefers-reduced-motion</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> no-preference<span class="token punctuation">)</span></span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@supports</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">scroll</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span></span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token selector">div</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
      <span class="token property">animation</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> spin linear both<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
      <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">view</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
      <span class="token property">animation-range</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> contain<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
    <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<figure class="wp-block-video"><video height="720" style="aspect-ratio: 1280 / 720;" width="1280" autoplay="" controls="" loop="" muted="" src="https://codrops-1f606.kxcdn.com/codrops/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/view-star.mp4?x72090" playsinline=""></video></figure>



<p class="has-text-align-center"><a href="https://codepen.io/argyleink/pen/wvOKbyL/2d672362df9ac37cf6920b5b6bc3a243">Try the demo</a></p>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">What just happened?!</h3>



<p>To the keyframes we defined, they still run from 0-100% 🤓 But now, 0% is when the element is entering the scroll area and 100% is when it’s about to go out of that scroll area.</p>



<p>This <a href="https://drafts.csswg.org/scroll-animations-1/#view-notation" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener"><code class="" data-line="">view()</code></a> function powers the animation as it crosses a scrollport. </p>



<p>The default value for <code class="" data-line="">animation-range</code> is <code class="" data-line="">cover</code>, which stinks in this star spinning demo because it makes it hard to distinguish between the <code class="" data-line="">scroll()</code> demo; it would be spinning the whole time you see it, as it would spin edge to edge. So I’ve also added <code class="" data-line="">animation-range</code> and set it to <code class="" data-line="">contain</code> so that the animation is edge to edge within the scrollport. It’s a little goofy looking that it’s so stiff as it enters and exits, but I hope it’s at least super clear what’s happening.</p>



<p>Bramus has built a super rad <a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/tools/view-timeline/ranges/" data-type="link" data-id="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/tools/view-timeline/ranges/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">tool</a> that helps visualize the options you can pass into <code class="" data-line="">animation-range</code>, definitely worth checking out if you intend to make some CSS scroll animations. </p>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">More elements!</h3>



<p>We saw one element animate itself across the viewport, but what if multiple elements get assigned the same animation and <code class="" data-line="">view()</code> attachment, but enter and exit the scrollport at different times? Let’s see!</p>



<p>In this next demo, each image scales up as it enters the scrollport, with individually running animations based on their unique scrollport intersection.</p>



<p>Instead of a keyword for the <code class="" data-line="">animation-range</code>, this time I show that you can use lengths. In this case, I wanted cute product images to scale up once they were 25vh in and complete the scaling by 75vh of their scrolling area:</p>



<figure class="wp-block-video"><video height="720" style="aspect-ratio: 1280 / 720;" width="1280" autoplay="" controls="" loop="" muted="" src="https://codrops-1f606.kxcdn.com/codrops/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/grid-view-scroll-effect.mp4?x72090" playsinline=""></video></figure>



<p class="has-text-align-center"><a href="https://codepen.io/argyleink/pen/RwvOmvY">Try the demo</a></p>



<p>The CSS for <strong>this effect is just</strong> <strong>this</strong>:</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@keyframes</span> scale-a-lil</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token selector">from</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">scale</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> .5<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span>
  
<span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@media</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token property">prefers-reduced-motion</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> no-preference<span class="token punctuation">)</span></span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token selector">figure img</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">animation</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> scale-a-lil linear both<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
    <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">view</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
    <span class="token property">animation-range</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> 25vh 75vh<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p>The keyframes set the out state for when scaled down. The animation says scale the images to regular size given the element intersects with the scrollport at the provided <code class="" data-line="">animation-range</code>.</p>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">More examples!</h2>



<p>With those building blocks of scroll attachment, view attachment and animation ranges, there’s <strong>a lot</strong> you can do. Let’s have a look at some more examples.</p>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Fade in the primary nav on scroll</h3>



<p><a rel="noreferrer noopener" href="https://codepen.io/argyleink/pen/KKrGNEO" target="_blank">This demo</a> has a very clean and minimal initial load where navigation is hidden, but then a little bit of scroll brings it in. It also shows how to animated the navbar shadow on scroll:</p>



<figure class="wp-block-video"><video height="720" style="aspect-ratio: 1280 / 720;" width="1280" autoplay="" controls="" loop="" muted="" src="https://codrops-1f606.kxcdn.com/codrops/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/revealing-header-nav.mp4?x72090" playsinline=""></video></figure>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Theme scroll</h3>



<p><a rel="noreferrer noopener" href="https://codepen.io/argyleink/pen/BaGVyyJ" target="_blank">This next demo</a> animates an angle <code class="" data-line="">@property</code> CSS variable, which is then used in a cylindrical color space. As the user scrolls, it makes a rainbow effect because the effect changes <code class="" data-line="">0turn</code> to <code class="" data-line="">1turn</code>; encompassing all the hues in the color space:</p>



<figure class="wp-block-video"><video height="720" style="aspect-ratio: 1280 / 720;" width="1280" autoplay="" controls="" loop="" muted="" src="https://codrops-1f606.kxcdn.com/codrops/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/scroll-hue.mp4?x72090" playsinline=""></video></figure>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Pull to refresh with scroll snap</h3>



<p>In <a rel="noreferrer noopener" href="https://codepen.io/argyleink/pen/ExOWjMe" data-type="link" data-id="https://codepen.io/argyleink/pen/ExOWjMe" target="_blank">this demo</a>, the pull-to-refresh icon rotates and the prompt text fades in as the user scrolls, effectively conveying the result of their gesture:</p>



<figure class="wp-block-video"><video height="720" style="aspect-ratio: 1280 / 720;" width="1280" autoplay="" controls="" loop="" muted="" src="https://codrops-1f606.kxcdn.com/codrops/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/ptr-snap-sdr.mp4?x72090" playsinline=""></video></figure>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">A more advanced example</h2>



<p>Up until now, examples have been with the whole page, and vertical scroll only. </p>



<p>In this section I will show you how to:</p>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li>Hookup <strong>horizontal</strong> <code class="" data-line="">scroll()</code> animation</li>



<li>Link animation with <strong>scroll-snap points</strong></li>



<li><strong>Share scroll() </strong>or<strong> view()</strong> progress with other elements with <code class="" data-line="">timeline-scope</code></li>
</ul>



<figure class="wp-block-video"><video height="1080" style="aspect-ratio: 1922 / 1080;" width="1922" autoplay="" controls="" loop="" muted="" src="https://codrops-1f606.kxcdn.com/codrops/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/carousel-full.mp4?x72090" playsinline=""></video></figure>



<p class="has-text-align-center"><a href="https://codepen.io/argyleink/pen/vYQQEmo">Try the demo</a></p>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Setting up snapping horizontal <code class="" data-line="">scroll()</code></h3>



<p>It’s time to pass some parameters to <code class="" data-line="">scroll()</code>. <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/animation-timeline/scroll" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">It is a function</a> after all 🤓</p>



<p>Attach the animation to horizontal scroll by specifying the axis in the function, such as <code class="" data-line="">scroll(x)</code>. Ensure that the axis matches the setup of your overflow. If you’re utilizing logical properties, you would use <code class="" data-line="">scroll(inline)</code>.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token selector">html</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token property">overflow-x</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> auto<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token property">scroll-snap-type</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> x mandatory<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">scroll</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>x<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>

  <span class="token selector">body > section</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">scroll-snap-align</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> start<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p>Now, any animation added to the markup will be controlled by the horizontal scroll of the page.</p>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Animating carousel cards in and out</h3>



<figure class="wp-block-video"><video height="1180" style="aspect-ratio: 1180 / 1180;" width="1180" autoplay="" controls="" loop="" muted="" src="https://codrops-1f606.kxcdn.com/codrops/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/carousel-cards-out.mp4?x72090" playsinline=""></video></figure>



<p>This is one of the best parts of the code to play with in the demo. It’s the animation as the cards go in and out of the horizontal scroll view: </p>



<figure class="wp-block-video"><video height="1180" style="aspect-ratio: 1180 / 1180;" width="1180" autoplay="" controls="" loop="" muted="" src="https://codrops-1f606.kxcdn.com/codrops/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/carousel-cards-in.mp4?x72090" playsinline=""></video></figure>



<p>The keyframes run from 0%-20% as elements “enter stage right”, then hold a natural state until 80% of the scroll area, then “exit stage left” finishing the 80%-100% part of the keyframes. My animation keyframes are like, “be shy as you enter the stage, then try to stick around as you exit the stage.” 😂</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@keyframes</span> fancy-in</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token comment">/* card entry */</span>
  <span class="token selector">0%</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">transform</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">translateX</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>25vw<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
  <span class="token comment">/* card on stage */</span>
  <span class="token selector">20%, 80%</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">opacity</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> 1<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
    <span class="token property">transform</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> none<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
  <span class="token comment">/* card exit */</span>
  <span class="token selector">100%</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">transform</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">translateX</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>90%<span class="token punctuation">)</span> <span class="token function">scale</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>.98<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
    <span class="token property">opacity</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> 0<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p>You should definitely mess with this code. Change the timing percentages or change the transforms.</p>



<p>Next, connect these dynamic keyframes to the scroll intersection on the x-axis for each <code class="" data-line="">.card</code> element. Ensure that the animation occurs only if motion is acceptable for the visitor.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@media</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token property">prefers-reduced-motion</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> no-preference<span class="token punctuation">)</span></span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@supports</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">scroll</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span></span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">animation</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> fancy-in linear both<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
    <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">view</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>x<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p>If you want to go off rails and play with what you’ve learned, try making the keyframes just 0%-100% and change the animation-range to get the effect. Or, <a href="https://developer.chrome.com/docs/css-ui/scroll-driven-animations#attaching_to_multiple_view_timeline_ranges_with_one_set_of_keyframes:~:text=it%20is%20also%20possible%20to%20create%20one%20set%20of%20keyframes%20that%20already%20contains%20the%20range%20information" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">add the animation ranges into the keyframes</a>. There are options here, and room for you to find a preference as an author and crafter.</p>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Crossfading the next and previous buttons with <code class="" data-line="">scroll()</code></h3>



<figure class="wp-block-video"><video height="426" style="aspect-ratio: 756 / 426;" width="756" autoplay="" controls="" loop="" muted="" src="https://codrops-1f606.kxcdn.com/codrops/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/carousel-button-controls-1.mp4?x72090" playsinline=""></video></figure>



<p>It’s a nice, elegant touch to visually fade the next button when at the end of a carousel, and also fade out a previous button if at the start. We can use CSS to do this! Here are the keyframes, they make this look like it might be simple:</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@keyframes</span> toggle-control</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token selector">50%</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> <span class="token property">opacity</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> 0 <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p>The next section is advanced and extremely important when you want to redirect an intersection of one element to power a different element’s animation. </p>



<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Define observable snap sections</h4>



<p>Rather than attempting to fade out the buttons with an estimated animation range for snap sections’ size, each button can observe a specific section and its intersection with the scrollport. This allows the buttons to be perfectly animated as the first and last sections go in and out of view. It’s awesome. </p>



<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Intrinsically sized snap area animation timelines</h4>



<p>Use <code class="" data-line="">view-timeline</code> to expose the <code class="" data-line="">view()</code> progress of a specific element. Provide a custom name and axis. Soon we’ll make these names more public to other elements, they can reference a sections view progress to power their own animation.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token selector">#section-1</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> <span class="token property">view-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-1 x <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token selector">#section-2</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> <span class="token property">view-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-2 x <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token selector">#section-3</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> <span class="token property">view-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-3 x <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token selector">#section-4</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> <span class="token property">view-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-4 x <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token selector">#section-5</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> <span class="token property">view-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-5 x <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token selector">#section-6</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> <span class="token property">view-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-6 x <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token selector">#section-7</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> <span class="token property">view-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-7 x <span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p>Next, we have to talk a little bit about the HTML of the carousel. The buttons and the section elements are distant siblings in the DOM tree. In order for the buttons to be able to observe the timelines of distant relatives scrollport intersection, it’s essential to raise the scope of each section’s timeline into a shared parent element. This is done with the <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/timeline-scope">timeline-scope</a> property.</p>



<p>Each section’s <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/view-timeline-name">view-timeline-name</a> is now available to all elements in <code class="" data-line=""><body></code>.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token selector">body</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token property">timeline-scope</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> 
    --section-1<span class="token punctuation">,</span> 
    --section-2<span class="token punctuation">,</span> 
    --section-3<span class="token punctuation">,</span> 
    --section-4<span class="token punctuation">,</span> 
    --section-5<span class="token punctuation">,</span> 
    --section-6<span class="token punctuation">,</span> 
    --section-7
  <span class="token punctuation">;</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p>I can now tell the previous (left arrow) button to fade out when the first section is showing, and tell the next (right arrow) button to fade out when the last section is showing.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token selector">.controls</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token selector">& > button</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token comment">/* if supported, enable the visibility toggling animation */</span>
    <span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@supports</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">scroll</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span></span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
      <span class="token property">animation</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> toggle-control linear both<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
    <span class="token punctuation">}</span>

    <span class="token comment">/* fade out the previous button when section 1 is in view */</span>
    <span class="token selector">&.previous</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
      <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-1<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
    <span class="token punctuation">}</span>

    <span class="token comment">/* fade out the next button when at the last section */</span>
    <span class="token selector">&.next</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
      <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-7<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
    <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p>The work to expose each sections timeline pays off in the next section, where more buttons want to know which section is snapped.</p>



<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Synchronizing the pagination dots with snapped carousel sections</h4>



<figure class="wp-block-video"><video height="426" style="aspect-ratio: 756 / 426;" width="756" autoplay="" controls="" loop="" muted="" src="https://codrops-1f606.kxcdn.com/codrops/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/carousel-pagination.mp4?x72090" playsinline=""></video></figure>



<p>First, we need some keyframes that represent being in and out of view. Since this is a <code class="" data-line="">view()</code> linked animation, 0% is out of view to the left, 100% is out of view to the right, and 50% is when the specified <code class="" data-line="">--section</code> by a pagination dot is snapped. </p>



<p>My keyframes chose scale and color to indicate a selected state.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@keyframes</span> dot-selected</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> 
  <span class="token selector">0%, 100%</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">scale</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> .75<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
  <span class="token selector">50%</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">scale</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> 1<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
    <span class="token property">background</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">var</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>--text-2<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p>My pagination consists of a series of links that target section IDs, which is advantageous because the browser handles scrolling elements into view, and they are focusable. The initial step is to apply our recently created animation to each dot.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token selector">.pagination > a</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@supports</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">scroll</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span></span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">animation</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> dot-selected linear both<span class="token punctuation">;</span>  
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p>Link each dot’s animation progress to a relevant section’s view timeline.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token selector">.pagination > a</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@supports</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">scroll</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span></span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">animation</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> dot-selected linear both<span class="token punctuation">;</span>  
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>

  <span class="token selector">&:nth-child(1)</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-1 <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
  <span class="token selector">&:nth-child(2)</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-2 <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
  <span class="token selector">&:nth-child(3)</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-3 <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
  <span class="token selector">&:nth-child(4)</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-4 <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
  <span class="token selector">&:nth-child(5)</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-5 <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
  <span class="token selector">&:nth-child(6)</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-6 <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
  <span class="token selector">&:nth-child(7)</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> --section-7 <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p>Now pagination dots restore to normal size and are a brighter color when their linked section’s <code class="" data-line="">view()</code> timeline is intersecting with the scroll area.</p>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Animating the theme with <code class="" data-line="">scroll()</code></h3>



<figure class="wp-block-video"><video height="1080" style="aspect-ratio: 1922 / 1080;" width="1922" autoplay="" controls="" loop="" muted="" src="https://codrops-1f606.kxcdn.com/codrops/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/carousel-full.mp4?x72090" playsinline=""></video></figure>



<p>This part is extra credit and just super fun. </p>



<p>The entire scroll area of the carousel is linked to a full turn of an angle named <code class="" data-line="">--hue</code>. As you scroll, the progress is converted into an angle between 0 and 1 turns. That angle is used as a hue variable inside an OKLCH color palette. </p>



<h4 class="wp-block-heading">Scroll linked hue angles</h4>



<p>The type safe casting of <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@property">@property</a> is what unlocks this.</p>


<strong>Browser Support:</strong> <ul class="browser-support"><li class="bs-chrome bs-supported"><span class="bs-browser">Chrome</span><span class="bs-info">Supported</span></li><li class="bs-firefox"><span class="bs-browser">Firefox</span><span class="bs-info">Not supported</span></li><li class="bs-ie"><span class="bs-browser">Internet Explorer</span><span class="bs-info">Not supported</span></li><li class="bs-safari"><span class="bs-browser">Safari</span><span class="bs-info">Not supported</span></li><li class="bs-opera"><span class="bs-browser">Opera</span><span class="bs-info">Not supported</span></li></ul>



<p>Here’s how to define a type safe custom property in CSS:</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@property</span> --hue</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token property">syntax</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token string">'<angle>'</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token property">initial-value</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> 0turn<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token property">inherits</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> false<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p>Use this typed property in some keyframes. The browser now knows to maintain the type when interpolating 0turn and 1turn, which also means it knows how to interpolate the value over time… or over scroll progress.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@keyframes</span> hue-cycle</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span> 
  <span class="token selector">to</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">--hue</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> 1turn<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p>Link the progress of our carousel scroll to the keyframes with just a few lines.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token selector">:root</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token property">animation</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> hue-cycle linear both<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">scroll</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>x<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p>Last, use the <code class="" data-line="">--hue</code> variable inside a color palette. I chose <code class="" data-line="">oklch</code> but you could also use <code class="" data-line="">hsl</code> or any hue angle accepting color space. My little theme has a couple of text colors, a couple of surface colors, a link color and a focus color. </p>



<p>As the hue rotates on scroll, all of these color variables update. </p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token selector">:root</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token comment">/* dynamic color props, hue powered by scroll */</span>
  <span class="token property">--surface-1</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">oklch</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>40% 50% <span class="token function">var</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>--hue<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token property">--surface-2</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">oklch</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>50% 40% <span class="token function">var</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>--hue<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token property">--text-1</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">oklch</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>98% 10% <span class="token function">var</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>--hue<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token property">--text-2</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">oklch</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>95% 20% <span class="token function">var</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>--hue<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token property">--link</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">oklch</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>99% 10% <span class="token function">var</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>--hue<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token property">--focus</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">oklch</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>80% 90% <span class="token function">var</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>--hue<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>

  <span class="token comment">/* fallback for if @property isnt supported */</span>
  <span class="token property">--hue</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> 275<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p>Add a neat gradient trick by leveraging <code class="" data-line="">background-attachment</code> to make a nice vignette radial gradient that changes color but doesn’t move on scroll. I also use <a href="https://developer.chrome.com/blog/css-relative-color-syntax">relative color syntax</a> for a quick one off variant from the palette, it’s 25% lighter than surface 2, resulting in that nice sunburst effect.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token selector">:root</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token property">background</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">radial-gradient</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>closest-corner circle<span class="token punctuation">,</span> 
    <span class="token function">oklch</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>from <span class="token function">var</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>--surface-2<span class="token punctuation">)</span> <span class="token function">calc</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>l * 1.25<span class="token punctuation">)</span> c h<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">,</span>
    <span class="token function">var</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>--surface-1<span class="token punctuation">)</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">)</span> fixed no-repeat<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Field notes</h2>



<p>Here are key takeaways of <code class="" data-line="">scroll()</code> and <code class="" data-line="">view()</code>.</p>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">CSS <code class="" data-line="">scroll()</code> recap</h3>



<p><strong>Features:</strong></p>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li>Links animation progress to any element with overflow auto, clip or scroll</li>



<li>Can use any <code class="" data-line="">scroll-timeline</code> named element, not just a nearest parent scroller, as long as <code class="" data-line="">timeline-scope</code> is used</li>



<li>Range can be customized to hone the animation timing</li>
</ul>



<p><strong>Great for:</strong></p>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/progress-bar/css/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Read progress</a></li>



<li>Scroll hints</li>



<li><a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/shrinking-header-shadow/css/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Sticky shadows</a></li>



<li>Carousels (<a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/cover-flow/css/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">cover flow</a>)</li>



<li><a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/reverse-scroll/css/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Reversals</a></li>
</ul>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">CSS <code class="" data-line="">view()</code> recap</h3>



<p><strong>Features:</strong></p>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li>An elements intersection with an element with overflow auto, clip or scroll</li>



<li>Can be any scroller</li>



<li>Can use any <code class="" data-line="">view-timeline</code> named element, as long as <code class="" data-line="">timeline-scope</code> is used</li>



<li>Range can be customized</li>



<li>Ranges are per element</li>



<li>Intersection is <a href="https://nerdy.dev/add-a-rad-gradient-progress-fill-to-a-range-input-with-no-JS" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">not limited to scroll gestures</a></li>



<li>Animation can be <a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/contact-list/css/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">customized based on entry and exit</a></li>
</ul>



<p><strong>Great for:</strong></p>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li>Introducing elements into a scroll area</li>



<li>Carousels</li>



<li><a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/cover-to-fixed-header/css/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Header</a> and footer tricks</li>



<li><a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/image-reveal/css/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Reveals</a></li>



<li><a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/stacking-cards/css/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Sticky tricks</a></li>
</ul>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Not covered in this article</h3>



<p>There’s syntax for combining keyframes and <code class="" data-line="">animation-range</code> into one 🤯</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@keyframes</span> animate-in-and-out</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token selector">entry 0%</span>  <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">opacity</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> 0<span class="token punctuation">;</span> <span class="token property">transform</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">translateY</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>100%<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
  <span class="token selector">entry 100%</span>  <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">opacity</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> 1<span class="token punctuation">;</span> <span class="token property">transform</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">translateY</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>0<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
  <span class="token selector">exit 0%</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">opacity</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> 1<span class="token punctuation">;</span> <span class="token property">transform</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">translateY</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>0<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
  <span class="token selector">exit 100%</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token property">opacity</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> 0<span class="token punctuation">;</span> <span class="token property">transform</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">translateY</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>-100%<span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token punctuation">}</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span>

<span class="token selector">#list-view li</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="token property">animation</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> animate-in-and-out linear both<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
  <span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">view</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<p><a href="https://developer.chrome.com/docs/css-ui/scroll-driven-animations#:~:text=it%20is%20also%20possible%20to%20create%20one%20set%20of%20keyframes%20that%20already%20contains%20the%20range%20information." target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Read all about it here from Bramus</a>, it’s slick.</p>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Respecting User Motion</h3>



<p>A good default user experience is a legible and static experience. </p>



<p>With the following media query, you are adding animations for users who have no indication via their device that they wish for reduced motion. I call this `<a href="https://open-props.style/#media-queries" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">motionOK</a>` when using <a href="https://drafts.csswg.org/mediaqueries-5/#at-ruledef-custom-media" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">custom media queries</a>.</p>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@media</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token property">prefers-reduced-motion</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> no-preference<span class="token punctuation">)</span></span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
  /* OK to add scroll motion * /
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading">Checking support</h3>



<pre class="wp-block-prismatic-blocks language-css" tabindex="0"><code class="language-css" data-line=""><span class="token atrule"><span class="token rule">@supports</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token property">animation-timeline</span><span class="token punctuation">:</span> <span class="token function">scroll</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span></span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
 
<span class="token punctuation">}</span></code></pre>

</ul>
